mn32:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling in the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood together with many distinguished elder disciples—
mn32:1.3: with Venerable Sāriputta, Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, Venerable Mahākassapa, Venerable Anuruddha, Venerable Revata, Venerable Ānanda, and other distinguished elder disciples.
mn32:10.1: Then Venerable Sāriputta said to those venerables:
mn32:10.2: “All of us have explained according to our own inspiration, friends.
mn32:10.3: Come, friends, let us go to the *Bhagavā*. Having gone to him, we will report this matter to the *Bhagavā*.
mn32:10.4: As the *Bhagavā* explains it to us, so we will remember it.”
mn32:10.5: “Yes, friend,” those venerables replied to Venerable Sāriputta.
mn32:10.6: Then those venerables went to the *Bhagavā*. Having gone to him, they paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, Venerable Sāriputta said to the *Bhagavā*:
mn32:11.1: “Here, *bhante*, Venerable Revata and Venerable Ānanda came to me to listen to the *dhamma* [*dhammassavanāya*].
mn32:11.10: for the uprooting of underlying tendencies [*anusayasamugghātāya*].
mn32:11.11: It is by such a *bhikkhu*, friend Sāriputta, that the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood would be beautified.’”
mn32:11.13: That is exactly as Ānanda, answering rightly, would answer.
mn32:11.14: For Ānanda, Sāriputta, is learned, one who retains what he has heard, a storehouse of what he has heard. Those *dhammas* that are good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-worded, which proclaim the perfectly complete and pure holy life—such *dhammas* are much heard by him, retained, recited verbally, examined by the mind [*citta*], and well penetrated by view.
mn32:11.15: He teaches the *dhamma* to the four assemblies with well-rounded words and phrases, in connected sequence, for the uprooting of underlying tendencies [*anusayasamugghātāya*].”
mn32:11.2: I saw Venerable Revata and Venerable Ānanda coming from far off.
mn32:11.3: Seeing them, I said to Venerable Ānanda:
mn32:11.5: Welcome to Venerable Ānanda, the *Bhagavā*’s attendant, one who moves near the *Bhagavā*.
mn32:11.6: Lovely, friend Ānanda, is the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood; the night is moonlit, the sal trees are all in full blossom, and divine fragrances, it seems, are wafting about.
mn32:11.7: What kind of *bhikkhu*, friend Ānanda, would beautify the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood?’
mn32:11.8: When this was said, *bhante*, Venerable Ānanda said to me:
mn32:11.9: ‘Here, friend Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* is learned, one who retains what he has heard …
mn32:12.1: “When this was said, *bhante*, I said to Venerable Revata:
mn32:12.10: That is exactly as Revata, answering rightly, would answer.
mn32:12.11: For Revata, Sāriputta, delights in seclusion and enjoys seclusion, is devoted inwardly to settling of the mind [*cetosamatha*], does not neglect meditation [*anirākatajjhāno*], is endowed with insight [*vipassanāya*], and frequents empty dwellings.”
mn32:12.2: ‘Venerable Ānanda has explained according to his own inspiration, friend Revata.
mn32:12.3: Now then, we ask Venerable Revata—
mn32:12.4: Lovely, friend Revata, is the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood; the night is moonlit, the sal trees are all in full blossom, and divine fragrances, it seems, are wafting about.
mn32:12.5: What kind of *bhikkhu*, friend Revata, would beautify the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood?’
mn32:12.6: When this was said, *bhante*, Venerable Revata said to me:
mn32:12.7: ‘Here, friend Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* delights in seclusion and enjoys seclusion, is devoted inwardly to settling of the mind [*cetosamatha*], does not neglect meditation [*anirākatajjhāno*], is endowed with insight [*vipassanāya*], and frequents empty dwellings.
mn32:12.8: It is by such a *bhikkhu*, friend Sāriputta, that the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood would be beautified.’”
mn32:13.1: “When this was said, *bhante*, I said to Venerable Anuruddha:
mn32:13.2: ‘Venerable Revata has explained according to his own inspiration …
mn32:13.3: What kind of *bhikkhu*, friend Anuruddha, would beautify the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood?’
mn32:13.4: When this was said, *bhante*, Venerable Anuruddha said to me:
mn32:13.5: ‘Here, friend Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, with the divine eye, purified and surpassing the human, surveys a thousand worlds.
mn32:13.6: Just as, friend Sāriputta, a person with good eyes …
mn32:13.7: it is by such a *bhikkhu*, friend Sāriputta, that the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood would be beautified.’”
mn32:13.8: “Sādhu, sādhu, Sāriputta. That is exactly as Anuruddha, answering rightly, would answer.
mn32:13.9: For Anuruddha, Sāriputta, with the divine eye, purified and surpassing the human, surveys a thousand worlds.”
mn32:14.1: “When this was said, *bhante*, I said to Venerable Mahākassapa:
mn32:14.10: For Kassapa, Sāriputta, is himself a forest-dweller and speaks in praise of forest-dwelling; he himself lives on almsfood and speaks in praise of living on almsfood; he himself wears rag-robes and speaks in praise of wearing rag-robes; he himself uses only three robes and speaks in praise of using only three robes; he himself has few wishes and speaks in praise of fewness of wishes; he himself is content and speaks in praise of contentment; he himself is secluded [*pavivitto*] and speaks in praise of seclusion [*viveka*]; he himself does not mix and speaks in praise of non-association; he himself has aroused energy and speaks in praise of arousing energy; he himself is accomplished in virtue and speaks in praise of accomplishment in virtue; he himself is accomplished in *samādhi* [*samādhisampanno*] and speaks in praise of accomplishment in *samādhi* [*samādhisampadāya*]; he himself is accomplished in discernment [*paññāsampanno*] and speaks in praise of accomplishment in discernment [*paññāsampadāya*]; he himself is accomplished in liberation and speaks in praise of accomplishment in liberation; he himself is accomplished in knowledge and vision of liberation and speaks in praise of accomplishment in knowledge and vision of liberation.”
mn32:14.2: ‘Venerable Anuruddha has explained according to his own inspiration, friend Kassapa.
mn32:14.3: Now then, we ask Venerable Mahākassapa …
mn32:14.4: What kind of *bhikkhu*, friend Kassapa, would beautify the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood?’
mn32:14.5: When this was said, *bhante*, Venerable Mahākassapa said to me:
mn32:14.6: ‘Here, friend Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* is himself a forest-dweller and speaks in praise of forest-dwelling; he himself lives on almsfood and speaks in praise of living on almsfood; … he himself wears rag-robes and speaks in praise of wearing rag-robes; … he himself uses only three robes and speaks in praise of using only three robes; … he himself has few wishes and speaks in praise of fewness of wishes; … he himself is content and speaks in praise of contentment; … he himself is secluded [*pavivitto*] and speaks in praise of seclusion [*viveka*]; … he himself does not mix and speaks in praise of non-association; … he himself has aroused energy and speaks in praise of arousing energy; … he himself is accomplished in virtue and speaks in praise of accomplishment in virtue; … he himself is accomplished in *samādhi* [*samādhisampanno*] and speaks in praise of accomplishment in *samādhi* [*samādhisampadāya*]; … he himself is accomplished in discernment [*paññāsampanno*] … he himself is accomplished in liberation … he himself is accomplished in knowledge and vision of liberation and speaks in praise of accomplishment in knowledge and vision of liberation.
mn32:14.7: It is by such a *bhikkhu*, friend Sāriputta, that the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood would be beautified.’”
mn32:14.9: That is exactly as Kassapa, answering rightly, would answer.
mn32:15.1: “When this was said, *bhante*, I said to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna:
mn32:15.2: ‘Venerable Mahākassapa has explained according to his own inspiration, friend Moggallāna.
mn32:15.3: Now then, we ask Venerable Mahāmoggallāna …
mn32:15.4: What kind of *bhikkhu*, friend Moggallāna, would beautify the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood?’
mn32:15.5: When this was said, *bhante*, Venerable Mahāmoggallāna said to me:
mn32:15.6: ‘Here, friend Sāriputta, two *bhikkhus* engage in a discussion on the *dhamma* [*abhidhammakathaṁ*]. They ask one another questions; when asked, they answer one another’s questions; they do not leave one another at a loss, and their discussion proceeds in line with the *dhamma*.
mn32:15.7: It is by such a *bhikkhu*, friend Sāriputta, that the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood would be beautified.’”
mn32:15.8: “Sādhu, sādhu, Sāriputta, that is exactly as Moggallāna, answering rightly, would answer.
mn32:15.9: For Moggallāna, Sāriputta, is a speaker on the *dhamma* [*dhammakathiko*].”
mn32:16.1: When this was said, Venerable Mahāmoggallāna said to the *Bhagavā*:
mn32:16.10: with whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in at midday, he dwells in that meditative attainment at midday;
mn32:16.11: with whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in during the evening, he dwells in that meditative attainment during the evening.
mn32:16.12: Just as, friend Moggallāna, a king or a chief minister might have a clothes chest full of cloths of various colors.
mn32:16.13: Whatever pair of garments he might wish to put on in the morning, that very pair of garments he would put on in the morning;
mn32:16.14: whatever pair of garments he might wish to put on at midday, that very pair of garments he would put on at midday;
mn32:16.15: whatever pair of garments he might wish to put on in the evening, that very pair of garments he would put on in the evening.
mn32:16.16: In just the same way, friend Moggallāna, a *bhikkhu* keeps mind [*citta*] under control and does not come under the control of mind [*citta*].
mn32:16.17: With whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in during the morning, he dwells in that meditative attainment during the morning;
mn32:16.18: with whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in at midday, he dwells in that meditative attainment at midday;
mn32:16.19: with whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in during the evening, he dwells in that meditative attainment during the evening.
mn32:16.20: It is by such a *bhikkhu*, friend Moggallāna, that the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood would be beautified.’”
mn32:16.22: That is exactly as Sāriputta, answering rightly, would answer.
mn32:16.23: For Sāriputta, Moggallāna, keeps mind [*citta*] under control and does not come under the control of mind [*citta*].
mn32:16.24: With whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in during the morning, he dwells in that meditative attainment during the morning;
mn32:16.25: with whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in at midday, he dwells in that meditative attainment at midday;
mn32:16.26: with whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in during the evening, he dwells in that meditative attainment during the evening.”
mn32:16.3: ‘All of us have explained according to our own inspiration, friend Sāriputta.
mn32:16.4: Now then, we ask Venerable Sāriputta—
mn32:16.5: Lovely, friend Sāriputta, is the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood; the night is moonlit, the sal trees are all in full blossom, and divine fragrances, it seems, are wafting about.
mn32:16.6: What kind of *bhikkhu*, friend Sāriputta, would beautify the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood?’
mn32:16.7: When this was said, *bhante*, Venerable Sāriputta said to me:
mn32:16.8: ‘Here, friend Moggallāna, a *bhikkhu* keeps mind [*citta*] under control and does not come under the control of mind [*citta*].
mn32:16.9: With whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in during the morning, he dwells in that meditative attainment during the morning;
mn32:17.1: When this was said, Venerable Sāriputta said to the *Bhagavā*:
mn32:17.10: The Greater Discourse at Gosiṅga, the second, is finished.
mn32:17.3: “All of you, Sāriputta, have spoken well, each in a way.
mn32:17.4: But now listen to me too as to what kind of *bhikkhu* would beautify the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood.
mn32:17.5: Here, Sāriputta, after the meal, on returning from the almsround, a *bhikkhu* sits down, folding his legs crosswise, setting his body erect, and establishing *sati* before him:
mn32:17.6: ‘I will not break this cross-legged seat until my mind [*citta*] is liberated from the *āsavas* through non-clinging/feeding [*anupādāya*].’
mn32:17.7: It is by such a *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, that the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood would be beautified.”
mn32:17.9: Those venerables were glad and rejoiced in what the *Bhagavā* had said.
mn32:2.1: Then Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, in the evening, having come out of seclusion, went to Venerable Mahākassapa. Having gone to him, he said:
mn32:2.2: “Come, friend Kassapa, let us go to Venerable Sāriputta to listen to the *dhamma* [*dhammassavanāya*].”
mn32:2.3: “Yes, friend,” Venerable Mahākassapa replied to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna.
mn32:2.4: Then Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, Venerable Mahākassapa, and Venerable Anuruddha went to Venerable Sāriputta to listen to the *dhamma* [*dhammassavanāya*].
mn32:3.1: Venerable Ānanda saw Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, Venerable Mahākassapa, and Venerable Anuruddha going to Venerable Sāriputta to listen to the *dhamma* [*dhammassavanāya*].
mn32:3.2: Seeing them, he went to Venerable Revata. Having gone to him, he said:
mn32:3.3: “Those good men are going, friend Revata, to Venerable Sāriputta to listen to the *dhamma* [*dhammassavanāya*].
mn32:3.4: Come, friend Revata, let us go to Venerable Sāriputta to listen to the *dhamma* [*dhammassavanāya*].”
mn32:3.5: “Yes, friend,” Venerable Revata replied to Venerable Ānanda.
mn32:3.6: Then Venerable Revata and Venerable Ānanda went to Venerable Sāriputta to listen to the *dhamma* [*dhammassavanāya*].
mn32:4.1: Venerable Sāriputta saw Venerable Revata and Venerable Ānanda coming from far off.
mn32:4.2: Seeing them, he said to Venerable Ānanda:
mn32:4.4: Welcome to Venerable Ānanda, the *Bhagavā*’s attendant, one who moves near the *Bhagavā*.
mn32:4.5: Lovely, friend Ānanda, is the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood; the night is moonlit, the sal trees are all in full blossom, and divine fragrances, it seems, are wafting about.
mn32:4.6: What kind of *bhikkhu*, friend Ānanda, would beautify the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood?”
mn32:4.7: “Here, friend Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* is learned, one who retains what he has heard, a storehouse of what he has heard. Those *dhammas* that are good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-worded, which proclaim the perfectly complete and pure holy life—such *dhammas* are much heard by him, retained, recited verbally, examined by the mind [*citta*], and well penetrated by view.
mn32:4.8: He teaches the *dhamma* to the four assemblies with well-rounded words and phrases, in connected sequence, for the uprooting of underlying tendencies [*anusayasamugghātāya*].
mn32:4.9: It is by such a *bhikkhu*, friend Sāriputta, that the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood would be beautified.”
mn32:5.1: When this was said, Venerable Sāriputta said to Venerable Revata:
mn32:5.2: “Venerable Ānanda has explained according to his own inspiration, friend Revata.
mn32:5.3: Now then, we ask Venerable Revata:
mn32:5.4: ‘Lovely, friend Revata, is the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood; the night is moonlit, the sal trees are all in full blossom, and divine fragrances, it seems, are wafting about.
mn32:5.5: What kind of *bhikkhu*, friend Revata, would beautify the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood?’”
mn32:5.6: “Here, friend Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* delights in seclusion and enjoys seclusion, is devoted inwardly to settling of the mind [*cetosamatha*], does not neglect meditation [*anirākatajjhāno*], is endowed with insight [*vipassanāya*], and frequents empty dwellings.
mn32:5.7: It is by such a *bhikkhu*, friend Sāriputta, that the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood would be beautified.”
mn32:6.1: When this was said, Venerable Sāriputta said to Venerable Anuruddha:
mn32:6.2: “Venerable Revata has explained according to his own inspiration, friend Anuruddha.
mn32:6.3: Now then, we ask Venerable Anuruddha:
mn32:6.4: ‘Lovely, friend Anuruddha, is the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood; the night is moonlit, the sal trees are all in full blossom, and divine fragrances, it seems, are wafting about.
mn32:6.5: What kind of *bhikkhu*, friend Anuruddha, would beautify the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood?’”
mn32:6.6: “Here, friend Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, with the divine eye, purified and surpassing the human, surveys a thousand worlds.
mn32:6.7: Just as, friend Sāriputta, a person with good eyes, having gone up to the upper terrace of a palace, might survey a thousand wheel-rims;
mn32:6.8: in just the same way, friend Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, with the divine eye, purified and surpassing the human, surveys a thousand worlds.
mn32:6.9: It is by such a *bhikkhu*, friend Sāriputta, that the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood would be beautified.”
mn32:7.1: When this was said, Venerable Sāriputta said to Venerable Mahākassapa:
mn32:7.2: “Venerable Anuruddha has explained according to his own inspiration, friend Kassapa.
mn32:7.3: Now then, we ask Venerable Mahākassapa:
mn32:7.4: ‘Lovely, friend Kassapa, is the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood; the night is moonlit, the sal trees are all in full blossom, and divine fragrances, it seems, are wafting about.
mn32:7.5: What kind of *bhikkhu*, friend Kassapa, would beautify the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood?’”
mn32:7.6: “Here, friend Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* is himself a forest-dweller and speaks in praise of forest-dwelling; he himself lives on almsfood and speaks in praise of living on almsfood; he himself wears rag-robes and speaks in praise of wearing rag-robes; he himself uses only three robes and speaks in praise of using only three robes; he himself has few wishes and speaks in praise of fewness of wishes; he himself is content and speaks in praise of contentment; he himself is secluded [*pavivitto*] and speaks in praise of seclusion [*viveka*]; he himself does not mix and speaks in praise of non-association; he himself has aroused energy and speaks in praise of arousing energy; he himself is accomplished in virtue and speaks in praise of accomplishment in virtue; he himself is accomplished in *samādhi* [*samādhisampanno*] and speaks in praise of accomplishment in *samādhi* [*samādhisampadāya*]; he himself is accomplished in discernment [*paññāsampanno*] and speaks in praise of accomplishment in discernment [*paññāsampadāya*]; he himself is accomplished in liberation and speaks in praise of accomplishment in liberation; he himself is accomplished in knowledge and vision of liberation and speaks in praise of accomplishment in knowledge and vision of liberation.
mn32:7.7: It is by such a *bhikkhu*, friend Sāriputta, that the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood would be beautified.”
mn32:8.1: When this was said, Venerable Sāriputta said to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna:
mn32:8.2: “Venerable Mahākassapa has explained according to his own inspiration, friend Moggallāna.
mn32:8.3: Now then, we ask Venerable Mahāmoggallāna:
mn32:8.4: ‘Lovely, friend Moggallāna, is the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood; the night is moonlit, the sal trees are all in full blossom, and divine fragrances, it seems, are wafting about.
mn32:8.5: What kind of *bhikkhu*, friend Moggallāna, would beautify the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood?’”
mn32:8.6: “Here, friend Sāriputta, two *bhikkhus* engage in a discussion on the *dhamma* [*abhidhammakathaṁ*]. They ask one another questions, and when asked, they answer one another’s questions; they do not leave one another at a loss, and their discussion proceeds in line with the *dhamma*.
mn32:8.7: It is by such a *bhikkhu*, friend Sāriputta, that the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood would be beautified.”
mn32:9.1: Then Venerable Mahāmoggallāna said to Venerable Sāriputta:
mn32:9.10: Just as, friend Moggallāna, a king or a chief minister might have a clothes chest full of cloths of various colors.
mn32:9.11: Whatever pair of garments he might wish to put on in the morning, that very pair of garments he would put on in the morning;
mn32:9.12: whatever pair of garments he might wish to put on at midday, that very pair of garments he would put on at midday;
mn32:9.13: whatever pair of garments he might wish to put on in the evening, that very pair of garments he would put on in the evening.
mn32:9.14: In just the same way, friend Moggallāna, a *bhikkhu* keeps mind [*citta*] under control and does not come under the control of mind [*citta*].
mn32:9.15: With whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in during the morning, he dwells in that meditative attainment during the morning;
mn32:9.16: with whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in at midday, he dwells in that meditative attainment at midday;
mn32:9.17: with whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in during the evening, he dwells in that meditative attainment during the evening.
mn32:9.18: It is by such a *bhikkhu*, friend Moggallāna, that the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood would be beautified.”
mn32:9.2: “All of us have explained according to our own inspiration, friend Sāriputta.
mn32:9.3: Now then, we ask Venerable Sāriputta:
mn32:9.4: ‘Lovely, friend Sāriputta, is the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood; the night is moonlit, the sal trees are all in full blossom, and divine fragrances, it seems, are wafting about.
mn32:9.5: What kind of *bhikkhu*, friend Sāriputta, would beautify the Gosiṅga sal-tree wood?’”
mn32:9.6: “Here, friend Moggallāna, a *bhikkhu* keeps mind [*citta*] under control and does not come under the control of mind [*citta*].
mn32:9.7: With whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in during the morning, he dwells in that meditative attainment during the morning;
mn32:9.8: with whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in at midday, he dwells in that meditative attainment at midday;
mn32:9.9: with whichever meditative attainment he wishes to dwell in during the evening, he dwells in that meditative attainment during the evening.
